algorithms.title: STEMI Reperfusion Decision Pathway
algorithms.description: Decision pathway from STEMI identification to reperfusion, including primary PCI and fibrinolysis pathways.
steps: Obtain and interpret 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes of first medical contact.
ST elevation or new LBBB consistent with STEMI identified.
Activate cardiac catheterization team immediately — FMC2D goal ≤90 minutes.
Administer aspirin 162–325 mg chewed.
Decision: Is primary PCI achievable within 90 minutes of FMC?
YES → Proceed to primary PCI (door-to-balloon ≤90 min).
NO → Is symptom onset <12 hours AND anticipated PPCI delay >120 minutes?
YES → Administer fibrinolysis (alteplase or tenecteplase): door-to-needle ≤30 min.
→ Assess for ≥50% ST-resolution at 60–90 minutes.
→ Successful → transfer to PCI-capable center for angiography within 3–24 hours.
→ Failed → emergent rescue PCI.
NO → Transfer to PCI-capable center as rapidly as possible; continue medical therapy.
Check right-sided leads V4R if inferior STEMI → if RV infarction, AVOID nitroglycerin.
Administer nitroglycerin for ongoing ischemic pain (CONTRAINDICATED: RV infarction, PDE5 inhibitor use within 24–48 h, SBP <90).
Add P2Y12 inhibitor (ticagrelor 180 mg or prasugrel 60 mg — prasugrel contraindicated with prior stroke/TIA or age ≥75).
Anticoagulate with unfractionated heparin (60 U/kg bolus, max 4,000 U, then 12 U/kg/hr).
Oxygen ONLY if SpO₂ <90%.
Morphine ONLY for refractory pain after nitroglycerin failure (caution: delays P2Y12 absorption).

algorithms.title: NSTEMI/Unstable Angina Risk-Stratified Management
algorithms.description: Risk-stratified approach to NSTEMI and unstable angina using TIMI/GRACE scores to guide timing of invasive strategy.
steps: Patient presents with ACS symptoms (chest pain, dyspnea) without ST elevation on ECG.
Obtain 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes.
No ST elevation → NSTEMI or unstable angina.
Immediate: aspirin 162–325 mg chewed + P2Y12 inhibitor (ticagrelor/prasugrel) + anticoagulation (heparin).
Risk stratify with TIMI and/or GRACE score.
HIGH RISK (recurrent angina, hemodynamic instability, dynamic ST/T changes, elevated troponin, GRACE >140, TIMI ≥3) → early invasive strategy within 24 hours.
INTERMEDIATE RISK → invasive strategy within 24–72 hours.
LOWER RISK → delayed invasive or ischemia-guided strategy (medical therapy + stress testing if needed).
Continue medical optimization: beta-blocker (if no contraindication), statin, ACE inhibitor as indicated.
Oxygen ONLY if SpO₂ <90%.
Serial troponins and ECGs to assess for evolution.
